none.justification: It correctly recognizes that the real scan-trigger logic is in driver_nl80211_scan.c and lists most substantive failure conditions, but it misses the actual driver_nl80211.c scan wrapper driver_nl80211_scan2 entirely and instead elevates nl80211_cmd_msg as the in-file function. That violates the GT’s key file-scope point, so it cannot receive full credit.

ccodegraph.justification: It explicitly identifies driver_nl80211_scan2 in driver_nl80211.c as a pure passthrough with no independent error conditions, and clearly separates the real scan failure logic in driver_nl80211_scan.c. The listed build, P2P, and kernel/AP-retry failure paths match the GT.

codegraph.justification: It gives the required file-scope answer: driver_nl80211.c contains only driver_nl80211_scan2, which has no error logic of its own. It then correctly labels the actual failure conditions as living in driver_nl80211_scan.c and enumerates the major distinct paths.

cbm.justification: It correctly states that driver_nl80211.c only has the thin driver_nl80211_scan2 wrapper and that the scan-trigger logic is in the separate driver_nl80211_scan.c translation unit. Its enumeration of message-build, P2P rate-mask, and send/AP fallback failures aligns with the GT.
